title: analytics — background jobs
description: 8 queued jobs covering capture, fan-out dispatch, per-provider delivery, batched flush, retry, identity reconciliation, retention purge, and sampling-rate reconciliation.

jobs.purpose: Dispatched by the AnalyticsEventCapturer when EmitsAnalyticsEvent trait fires inline (rare) OR when a domain event's registered capturer chooses async capture. Standard sync capture goes through AnalyticsEventCapturer::capture() directly which creates the row synchronously; this job is the async escape hatch.
steps: Read the domain-event snapshot from the job payload.
Resolve subject via AnalyticsIdentity or User lookup.
Snapshot attribution from AttributionContext (frozen from job dispatch time — the request context is gone).
Snapshot consent via ConsentGate::allows(subject, 'analytics').
Compute eligible_provider_count via AnalyticsProviderConfig fan-out with sampling roll.
Insert AnalyticsEvent row — observer fires AnalyticsEventCaptured.
jobs.uniqueVia: (domain_event_id, capturer_class) when domain_event_id is available; else the job's random ULID

jobs.purpose: Reads an AnalyticsEvent row + fans out DispatchToProviderJob per eligible provider. Skips providers with circuit_breaker_state.state='open'. Suppresses providers where consent has since revoked (re-checks consent_snapshot column).
steps: Load AnalyticsEvent by ID.
Load active AnalyticsProviderConfigs for tenant where enabled_event_types matches event_name.
For each config: check circuit_breaker_state; skip if open. Check consent_snapshot; suppress if revoked. Check sampling roll (stable hash); suppress if rolled out.
Insert AnalyticsDelivery(status=pending) per eligible provider.
Dispatch DispatchToProviderJob per delivery (or add to batch buffer for batching providers).
Fire AnalyticsEventDispatched.

jobs.queue: resolved-at-dispatch — analytics-critical if event.category IN (funnel_step, lifecycle), else analytics
jobs.purpose: Single-event delivery to a single provider (non-batching path OR opportunistic non-batched dispatch when the batch queue is otherwise empty).
steps: Load AnalyticsDelivery(id) + related AnalyticsEvent + AnalyticsProviderConfig.
Resolve AnalyticsProviderManager->instance(provider).
Call ProviderPayloadTransformer::transform(event, provider) to produce native payload.
Fire AnalyticsDeliveryAttempted.
HTTP POST to provider endpoint.
On 2xx: update AnalyticsDelivery(status=delivered, response_http_status, latency_ms, response body capped at 8KB). Fire AnalyticsDeliverySucceeded.
On 429: update AnalyticsDelivery(status=rate_limited, next_retry_at=now + Retry-After). Fire AnalyticsDeliveryRateLimited.
On 5xx / network: update AnalyticsDelivery(status=failed, attempt_number+=1, next_retry_at=now + backoff[attempt]). Fire AnalyticsDeliveryFailed.
On non-retryable (4xx except 429): update AnalyticsDelivery(status=permanently_failed). Fire AnalyticsDeliveryFailed.
jobs.notes: Retries are HANDLED VIA RetryFailedAnalyticsDeliveriesJob (schedule-driven), not via Laravel's job retry mechanism. This gives us explicit control over the next_retry_at column + backoff schedule.

jobs.purpose: Per-provider batch flush. Dispatched by the batch buffer when batch_config.size reached OR flush_interval_ms elapsed OR shutdown signal received.
steps: Load buffered AnalyticsDelivery(status=pending, batch_id=<this batch>) rows.
Concatenate ProviderPayloadTransformer output into the provider's batch shape.
Fire AnalyticsDeliveryAttempted per delivery.
HTTP POST batch to provider batch endpoint.
Parse provider batch response — many providers return per-event success/failure detail (Segment does; Amplitude returns overall).
Update each AnalyticsDelivery row to delivered / failed based on the response detail.
Fire AnalyticsBatchFlushed on total success OR AnalyticsBatchFailed on transport failure.
Fire per-delivery events (AnalyticsDeliverySucceeded / Failed).
jobs.uniqueVia: (tenant_id, provider_config_id) — one flush job in flight per (tenant, provider)

jobs.purpose: Every 30 seconds — scan AnalyticsDelivery where status IN (failed, rate_limited) AND next_retry_at <= now() AND attempt_number < config.retry_config.max_attempts. Dispatch DispatchToProviderJob to retry.
jobs.notes: Scheduled every 30s. Reads a bounded batch (100 rows per invocation) to avoid overwhelming providers on recovery.

jobs.purpose: Nightly 03:00 UTC per tenant. Cleans dangling anonymous identities: (a) purges AnalyticsIdentity rows with last_seen_at older than 90 days and identified_user_id IS NULL; (b) rewrites subject_id on any remaining anonymous events whose subject is now identified but MergeAliasedEventsJob failed to run (belt-and-braces); (c) purges identified identities where the User was erased more than 30 days ago.

Weekly Sunday 03:00 UTC. Retention purge: analytics_events > 2 years (5 with extended retention); analytics_deliveries > 90 days; analytics_provider_configs deleted_at > 90 days. Writes a summary row into audit + fires per-batch counters.
jobs.notes: Ordering matters: deliveries first (they RESTRICT event deletion), then events, then configs.

jobs.purpose: Nightly 04:00 UTC. Computes per-event actual dispatch rate vs configured sampling_rate for the past 24h. Emits a monitoring metric per provider per event_name so ops can see if sampling is behaving as configured. Not a data-mutating job — diagnostic only.
